hero.title: Ushering in the Next Era of Indian Music
hero.subtitle: Svarit is dedicated to the preservation and promotion of Indian classical music through education, performance, and community engagement.

about.heading: Our Mission is to Inspire Young & Fresh Perspectives to Reshape Indian Music
about.subheading: Svarit was founded in 2001 with the vision of promoting and propagating the rich tradition of Hindustani Raag Sangeet, as envisioned by the legendary vocalist Pandit Dinkar Kaikini.
paragraphs: Svarit, a Sanskrit word meaning “ascending towards the divine”, embodies our belief in music as a pathway to higher artistic and spiritual expression. Founded to preserve and promote the legacy of Indian Music as envisioned by Pandit Dinkar Kaikini, Svarit is rooted in a rich musical lineage that traces through the legacies of the great masters of Hindustani classical music, the likes of Vishnu Narayan Bhatkhande, Ustad Faiyaz Khan, S. N. Ratanjankar, Ustad Allarakha Khan & Ustad Zakir Hussain.

Today, under the guidance of Pt Yogesh Samsi, Svarit is more than a trust. It is a vibrant cultural platform where tradition meets innovation. Through concerts, festivals, educational initiatives, artist development, and collaborative projects, we nurture excellence while ensuring our country's arts remain relevant, accessible, and inspiring for future generations. Svarit continues to celebrate heritage, foster creativity, and shape the future of Indian Music.

initiatives.heading: Engaging Through More than 2 Decades of Keeping Tradition Alive

events.title: Dinarang Smriti
events.description: Since 2011, Svarit has presented the annual Dinarag Smriti Festival in memory of Pandit Dinkar Kaikini.

events.title: Samprati - Kathak Festival
events.description: Svarit also supported Samprati, a Kathak dance festival presented in Mumbai by acclaimed kathak dancer Smt Mansi Deshpande.

events.title: Chatur Sujan Dinarang Ek Parampara
events.description: Svarit also supported Samprati, a Kathak dance festival presented in Mumbai by acclaimed kathak dancer Smt Mansi Deshpande.

workshops.title: Hindustani Vocal Workshop
workshops.description: An intensive workshop on Hindustani raag sangeet led by senior disciples of the Agra Gharana.

workshops.title: Tabla & Rhythm Workshop
workshops.description: A focused session on rhythm, taal, and tabla accompaniment for classical vocalists.

donate.heading: Join us in shaping the future of Indian Music.

contact.heading: We'd Love to Hear from You
contact.description: We are a community of dreamers & changemakers. What began as a family endeavour is now expanding to welcome fresh perspectives. If you resonate with our mission, please get in touch.

footer.address: Anandashram, 22 Pandita Ramabai Rd, Gamdevi, Mumbai - 400 007
